title: The parity-violating asymmetry including QED corrections in high-energy electron-nucleus collisions
abstract: The parity-violating asymmetry, accounting for the vector and axial-vector vertex plus self-energy correction as well as for vacuum polarization, is calculated nonperturbatively by solving the corresponding Dirac equation for the electronic scattering states. Investigating the nuclei $^{27}$Al, $^{48}$Ca and $^{208}$Pb at collision energies in the GeV region and at forward scattering angles matching the experimental geometries, it is found that the combined QED effects change the parity-violating asymmetry by less than one percent. The same is true for $^{12}$C and $^{208}$Pb at an energy of 150 MeV.
